serve-options.description: Set BeSpunky house dev-server options (host 0.0.0.0, poll 1000) on a project's serve target.

devcontainer.description: Generate the BeSpunky-standard .devcontainer/devcontainer.json (Claude CLI + extension, .claude persistence, plugin pre-install).

claude-settings.description: Generate .claude/settings.json wiring the claude-toolkit + nx marketplaces (autoUpdate on); keep .claude/data out of git.

angular-ai.description: Wire Angular's official AI tooling into the project. Writes project-scoped .mcp.json declaring the Angular CLI MCP server (`npx -y @angular/cli mcp`, always-latest) with its DEFAULT knowledge tools (get_best_practices, search_documentation, find_examples, ai_tutor, onpush_zoneless_migration); the experimental exec tools (build/devserver/test/e2e) are deliberately omitted because an integrated Nx workspace has no root angular.json and Nx owns those targets. Merges into any existing .mcp.json (preserves user-added servers). The Angular agent skills (angular-developer, angular-new-app) are NOT vendored here — post-create.sh fetches them fresh from github.com/angular/skills into .claude/skills/ on every container build; this generator just keeps that cache out of git.

firebase-emulators.description: Scaffold Firebase emulator config + Nx targets + app initialization using Angular's canonical environment-files pattern. Writes firebase.json (emulators block + singleProjectMode); deliberately does NOT write .firebaserc (cloud-project linkage is the Firebase CLI's job — `firebase use --add`). Generates src/environments/{environment.interface.ts, environment.ts, environment.prod.ts} (the latter two only if absent — your real production web config is preserved), writes src/app/firebase.config.ts (only when absent or in the legacy ngDevMode-and-two-consts shape; migrates non-empty productionFirebaseConfig values into environment.prod.ts before rewriting; leaves modern files alone to preserve user customizations), registers the environment.ts → environment.prod.ts fileReplacements on the production build configuration, wires app.config.ts, adds `emulators` + `emulators:<svc>` targets (each passes --project=demo-<name> so emulators work without login), and wraps `serve` to launch the suite alongside the app.

playwright.description: Add @playwright/test as a devDependency so the devcontainer's post-create.sh installs Chromium + apt deps on container build. The browser binary lives in a per-workspace Docker volume so rebuilds don't re-download. Pairs with the `browser-automation:playwright` skill which describes how to use it. Always-on for BeSpunky projects — no opt-in flag.

mark-extractable.description: Mark an existing Nx library as a reusable-tool extraction candidate: add the `reusable-tool:extraction-candidate` tag and write an extraction.json marker (proposed @bespunky package name, kind, declared deps, and installed framework version ranges) so the host-side extract-tool can lift it into the shared workspace later. Sandbox-safe (touches only this workspace). PREREQUISITE: structure the generic code into its own clean Nx library first — this marks it, it doesn't abstract it.

adopt-extracted.description: Close the extraction loop: replace a local library (already published as @bespunky/<name>) with the public-npm package. Honours verify-then-delete — the default run adds the package + rewrites imports but keeps the lib; build to verify, then re-run with --finalize to remove the now-unused local lib. --keepShim instead leaves the lib as a thin re-export for staged migration.

navigation-core.description: Scaffold the self-contained navigation-core kernel — the reusable base that domain-specific navigation builds on (the engineering:typed-reactive-navigation pattern). VENDORS navigation-x verbatim (@bespunky/angular-zen/router-x/navigation): routeConfigFor / provideRouterX / useNavigationX / RouteComposer / strong Router typing (the auto-derived typed navigator). On top, BeSpunky reusable additions navigation-x doesn't yet ship: the BsNavLink directive that AUGMENTS Angular RouterLink via hostDirectives (inherits the real href + every RouterLink feature — queryParams/fragment/target/relative/RouterLinkActive — and adds only a typed composer binding + a `navigated` telemetry seam; it does not reimplement RouterLink), useNavigationLinks (the composer-value twin of useNavigationX, for binding links), RouteState (URL read-side signals), a typed EventBus + bindEvents, and navigation middleware. Files are copied byte-for-byte (no tokens, no formatting) to stay faithful to upstream; treat lib/* except extras/ as vendored. Adds @bespunky/typescript-utils (the kernel's type-util dependency). Run once per workspace: `nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:navigation-core [--directory=...]` (default libs/navigation-core/src).

domain-navigation.description: Scaffold a domain's THIN navigation config — the per-domain layer of the engineering:typed-reactive-navigation pattern, on top of the navigation-core kernel (run `nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:navigation-core` once first). Emits ONLY what is domain-specific: the typed route tree (routeConfigFor<Entity>().route(... as const)), the entity + event union + a domain EventBus<Event> subclass, the navigation file (useNavigationX/useNavigationLinks wrappers + the PURE event->navigator mapper + binding + provideX), and domain selectors extending RouteState. The composer, auto-derived navigator, the BsNavLink directive (real href so copy-link/open-in-new-tab survive), the event bus and the selectors base are INHERITED from navigation-core — never regenerated. After running, point the `@navigation-core` import at your lib (path alias or relative). Run as `nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:domain-navigation <domain> [--directory=...]`; files land in <directory|libs/<name>/src/lib>/navigation/.

publishable-lib.description: Scaffold a BeSpunky-standard publishable library. Delegates to @nx/angular:library (or @nx/js:library when --nonAngular) then post-processes to the house standard: ng-packagr FESM2022 build to dist/{projectRoot}, Vitest unit tests (unitTestRunner vitest-angular), a per-project `nx release` block (version.currentVersionResolver git-tag + fallbackCurrentVersionResolver disk) and nx-release-publish.packageRoot dist/{projectRoot}, modern nested ng-package.json (entryFile src/index.ts, NO umdModuleIds), standalone-only (skipModule), prefix bs. Authors the lib's OWN package.json (incl. any workspace:* sibling deps) and adds the ./<dir>/<name> entry to the root tsconfig.json references[] — linking is package-manager workspaces (workspace:*) + TS project references, NO tsconfig.base.json path aliases (nx sync maintains references). --nonAngular switches to a plain-TS @nx/js:tsc library with NO ng-package.json (typescript-utils/rxjs). Never touches nx.json (root release/releaseTag config is Foundation's job).

secondary-entrypoint.description: Add a secondary entry point to a publishable library: packages/<lib>/<name>/{ng-package.json (entryFile src/index.ts), src/index.ts}, resolving as the @bespunky/<lib>/<name> subpath under the package-manager workspace symlink (workspaces + project references — NO tsconfig.base.json path alias). Generalized from xpand's design-system-secondary-entrypoint: delegates to @nx/angular's librarySecondaryEntryPointGenerator (skipModule), STRIPS any tsconfig.base.json path alias the base generator adds, and normalizes the nested ng-package.json. --component=standalone optionally scaffolds a standalone component and re-exports it; default --component=none leaves an empty src/index.ts the consumer fills. Suite is standalone-only (no scam).

wiki-summary.description: Regenerate a library wiki's summary.json index (the compodoc `includes` SUMMARY). Devkit port of the legacy generate-wiki-summary Node script: walks the wiki folder via the Nx Tree (dry-run-safe, not raw fs), honoring per-folder .order files (split on /\r?\n/ — LF/CRLF-tolerant, fixing the old CRLF-only bug), building an ordered tree of { title, file, children? } where title = filename with dashes replaced by spaces and a folder gets children only when a sibling <folder>.md exists. Default wikiFolder packages/<lib>/wiki. Intended to also be wired as a Nx target in the docs build so summary.json regenerates in CI.
